health_py_handlers.response_shape: Prometheus text exposition (generate_latest(REGISTRY)) with CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST media
{"status": "ready"|"not_ready", "ready": bool, "components": {...}}; 503 with detail listing failed components when not ready

health_canary_py_handlers.response_shape: CanaryHealthResponse: {status, version, timestamp, deployment:{type,image_tag,start_time,uptime_seconds}, components, integration, metrics (canary only)}
{"status": "ready"|"not_ready", "message": "database not available"}; placeholder db=None always returns ready

common_response: {"status": <healthy|ready|alive|unhealthy|not_ready|degraded>, "version": str, "service": str, "components": {<component>: {status, message?, last_check?, ...}}}; failures surface as 503 JSONResponse with detail listing failed components
prometheus_metrics: generate_latest(REGISTRY) — full Prometheus default registry exposition (CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST)
process metrics emitted via prometheus_client default collectors
CanaryMetrics: request_count, error_count, error_rate, avg_latency_ms, p95_latency_ms, p99_latency_ms, memory_usage_mb, cpu_usage_percent (placeholder zeros in current implementation)
liveness_logic: Pure static: /health returns fixed {status:healthy,version:1.0.0,service:TraceRTM API} and /health/liveness returns {status:alive}; no dependency probing, suitable as Kubernetes liveness probe.
readiness_logic: Readiness probes critical dependencies only. /ready calls check_database(db) and check_redis(cache); ready iff every component.status == 'healthy', else returns 503 with detail listing failed components. /health/readiness (canary router) is a stub: db is hardcoded None, so it always returns {status:ready} without actually probing. /api/v1/health is the comprehensive probe (db, cache, nats_client, go backend, migrations) returning 503 if any component unhealthy.
